right_target.finding: OpenTargets returns 1172 melanoma evidence rows for MTOR; Cancer Gene Census flags MTOR as a somatic-mutation driver (score 1.0, resourceScore 1) in cutaneous, superficial-spreading, nodular and desmoplastic melanoma, plus EuropePMC literature (resourceScore 128). GWAS catalog maps 50 SNPs to the MTOR locus but none are melanoma trait-associated (catalog variants only, e.g. rs77270747 intron_variant).
right_target.provenance: OpenTargets_get_evidence_by_datasource (MONDO_0005105, ENSG00000198793, 1172 evidences); gwas_get_snps_for_gene (MTOR, 50 SNPs, no melanoma trait)

right_tissue.finding: CellMarker returns only 2 human MTOR marker records, neither in melanocyte/skin/melanoma tissue (Beta cell in Blood; Multipotent progenitor cell in Stomach). No melanoma-specific tissue enrichment signal available.

right_safety.finding: MTOR is broadly essential/ubiquitous (CellMarker normal-cell records: pancreatic beta cell, gastric progenitor). OpenFDA lists 16850 adverse-event reports for everolimus, an approved mTOR-inhibitor immunosuppressant (pharm_class: mTOR Inhibitor Immunosuppressant [EPC], Decreased Immunologic Activity [PE]); example serious reactions include stomatitis and epilepsy. Systemic immunosuppression and on-target toxicity are the dominant liabilities.

right_patient.finding: 7 melanoma trials of mTOR inhibitors (everolimus/temsirolimus/nab-rapamycin), ALL COMPLETED or TERMINATED (e.g. NCT00521001, NCT01014351, NCT00591734 Phase 2; NCT01166126 TERMINATED); every use is combination/investigational and none reached approval. No MTOR-directed drug is in active clinic for melanoma at threshold. MTOR is a NEGATIVE for melanoma specifically.
right_patient.provenance: search_clinical_trials (intervention=MTOR OR everolimus, condition=melanoma, total_count=7, all COMPLETED/TERMINATED)

right_commercial.finding: No commercial melanoma program: Thera-SAbDab returns 0 antibody therapeutics targeting MTOR, and all 7 melanoma trials are completed/terminated Phase 1-2 combinations with no active or approved melanoma indication. mTOR inhibitors are commercialized for other indications (renal, breast, TSC) but not melanoma.
right_commercial.provenance: TheraSAbDab_search_by_target (MTOR, count=0); search_clinical_trials (7 trials, none active/approved for melanoma)

tractability.finding: MTOR is highly tractable: Pharos target development level Tclin (clinically drugged), family Kinase, UniProt P42345, novelty 0.00012 (well-studied), publicationCount 2295. Multiple approved small-molecule mTOR inhibitors exist.
